name: Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown ground beef with onion and salt and pepper if you like.  Drain.
Stir in ketchup, brown sugar, and mustard.
Bring up to a simmer and allow to cook for five minutes more.
I serve mine on hamburger buns that are toasted with just a little bit of additional mustard on the bun.

name: Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown the ground meat and onions, drain off fat.
Stir in remaining ingredients, mix thoroughly.  Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.

name: Sloppy Joes...sort Of...
directions: The 'mixture': In a food processor, mince together all garlic cloves, onions, worchestershire, sazon and fruit juice.  Mince well.
Add oil and ground beef to a large pot.  When the meat is almost fully cooked, add half of your 'mixture'.  Fully cook the meat.  Drain all fluid from the pot.
Return the pot to the flame, add the remaining portion of your 'mixture'.  Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Before serving, squeeze a bit of ketchup on top of the meat.
**I've been known to buy pre-made croissant rolls and fill them with the meat mixture.
If you choose to do this, you MUST squeeze all the oil and liquid from the meat first.
Enjoy!

name: Chipotle Sloppy Joes
directions: Cook ground beef, onion, and bell pepper in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat, until meat is brown and vegetables are tender.
Drain off excess fat.
Stir in tomato sauce, brown sugar, salt, and Chipotle Sauce.
Cover and simmer 30 to 40 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Maidrites ( Sloppy Joes )
directions: Brown ground beef. Combine catsup, worcestershire sauce, vinegar, b.sugar, & tomato sauce. Add to ground beef and simmer for 15 minutes.
This freezes well.

name: Sloppy Joe Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients.  Makes enough sauce for 1-1/2 to 2 pounds of ground beef.

name: Sloppy Joe Tacos
directions: Brown hamburger and small onion.
Add ketchup, sugar, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and mustard. Simmer covered for 30 minutes.
Add mixture to warm taco shells and top with cheddar cheese.

name: Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown ground beef with onion. Drain off fat.
Mix Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, mustard and ketchup into meat mixture.
Mix Flour and water together with wire whisk, mix into meat mixture.
Bring meat mixture to a boil and then simmer for 30 minute or cook in a crock pot.

name: Quick & Easy Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown meat in a skillet until it has just cooked through.  Add in salt and pepper.
Drain meat onto paper towels so that the grease and fat have been soaked up.
Using the same skillet (after it has been wiped down with the a paper towel), combine all the remaining ingredients and bring to a slight simmer.
Add the meat back along with some fresh cracked black pepper.  (You can also add the meat after 3-4 minutes of slight simmering if you prefer very soft onions.).
Heat thoroughly and serve on a steamed or toasted bun with kettle chips.
These are also great topped with cheese and a hot dog.

name: Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown ground beef and onion till done, onions will be clear and the beef will be browned.
Drain off the fat.
Add in remaining ingredients and cook till well blended and heated throughly.
Serve on buns with french fries.

name: Teriyaki Sloppy Joes
directions: brown the beef and the garlic, drain the fat.
add the carrots and onion, cook 3-4 minutes.
turn heat to low, add the tomato sauce and terriyaki.
simmer for 15 minutes.

name: Sloppy Joes
directions: Brown meat in frying pan and drain. Add the rest of the ingredients and heat through.

name: Sloppy Joe's
directions: Brown ground beef in skillet with onion and green pepper.  Drain.
Add to crock pot with all remaining ingredients.
Cook on LOW for 6-8 hours.  Stir occasionally.
